title: 10 best herbal teas for eczema

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, carotenoids, and triterpenoids, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritation associated with eczema.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea, such as lutein and zeaxanthin, can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and skin damage, thereby reducing the severity of eczema symptoms.
Soil-up agents: Pot marigold tea has been shown to have a soothing effect on the skin, helping to lock in moisture and reduce dryness, which is common in eczema sufferers.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, teas helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tea's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling. Additionally, calendula's ability to promote wound healing and tissue repair helps to reduce the severity of eczema symptoms, including dryness and itching. This natural remedy provides relief and comfort for those suffering from eczema, allowing the skin to heal and recover more efficiently.
remedy_constituents: Carotenoids: These pigments have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with eczema.

Phenolic acids (such as ferulic acid): These compounds exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help to reduce inflammation, oxidative stress, and skin damage in eczema patients.

Saponins: These triterpenoid glycosides have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce skin inflammation, prevent infections, and promote wound healing in eczema-prone skin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm eczema symptoms.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making them beneficial for eczema treatment.
Stems: They are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate eczema symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried calendula officinalis flowers from a clean location to use for tea.
Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine mesh sieve into a large cup.
Add honey to taste to sweeten the tea and adjust to desired consistency.
Serve the tea immediately and enjoy its potential health benefits after 10-15 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids, terpenes, and lignans that help reduce inflammation in the skin, which can alleviate symptoms of eczema.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in stinging nettle tea neutralize free radicals and protect the skin from damage caused by oxidative stress, which can exacerbate eczema.
Hormone regulation: Stinging nettle tea has been shown to help regulate hormone imbalances, which is important for managing eczema as hormonal fluctuations can trigger or worsen symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, teas helps with eczema because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's rich antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and promote healing, which can alleviate eczema symptoms such as redness and itching. Additionally, stinging nettle tea is believed to have antimicrobial properties that help combat infections that can exacerbate eczema. Regular consumption of this herbal tea may provide relief and promote overall skin health.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids (especially stachydrine and caffeic acid derivatives): These compounds may help reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema by suppressing the activity of various inflammatory mediators and enzymes.

Flavonoids (especially quercetin and kaempferol): These flavonoids have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing the symptoms of eczema, such as redness and swelling.

Phenolic acids (especially chlorogenic and caffeic acids): These phenolic acids have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation, itching, and skin damage associated with eczema.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema.
Stems: They are also a good source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, similar to the leaves, and are often used to make teas for eczema relief.
Roots: They are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them useful for soothing and calming skin irritations caused by eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of urtica dioica when they are young and tender in the spring or early summer.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the leaves finely with kitchen shears or a sharp knife to release their medicinal properties.
Steep one teaspoon of chopped leaves in a cup of boiling water for five to ten minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to enjoy the herbal remedy as a warm or cold infusion.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation in the skin, a key factor in alleviating eczema symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: The tea's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eczema flare-ups and worsen existing symptoms.
Immunomodulatory effects: Purple coneflower tea has been shown to modulate the immune system, helping to reduce excessive inflammation and prevent further irritation in people with eczema.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, teas helps with eczema because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's flavonoids and phenolic compounds work to reduce redness and swelling, while its alkaloids help to modulate the immune response. This helps to alleviate the symptoms of eczema, such as itching and dryness, and promotes a healthier skin barrier. By incorporating Echinacea purpurea tea into your daily routine, you may experience a reduction in eczema flare-ups and improved overall skin well-being.
remedy_constituents: Ishaginin: This alkylamide has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of eczema symptoms by reducing inflammation and itching.
Cichoric acid: A phenolic compound, cichoric acid has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe and protect the skin from eczema flare-ups.

Echinacoside: A phenylethanoid glycoside, echinacoside has been found to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the body's immune response and reduce eczema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea roots are commonly used as they contain a higher concentration of alkaloids and glycosides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Leaves: Echinacea leaves are often used to make teas as they contain flavonoids and caffeic acid, which may help reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations.

Flowers: Echinacea flowers are popular in teas due to their rich content of flavonoids, anthocyanins, and phenolic acids, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve skin health, and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and roots in the early morning or late afternoon.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea flowers and roots in a single layer for 7 to 10 days.
Crush 2 to 4 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and roots into a fine powder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the powder in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool before serving to enjoy its potential health benefits.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.

Soothing and moisturizing properties: Aloe vera's gel contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which provide intense hydration and relief to dry, itchy skin affected by eczema.

Antimicrobial properties: The antibacterial and antifungal compounds in aloe vera help prevent secondary infections that can exacerbate eczema symptoms, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further irritation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, teas helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory properties. It soothes and calms irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with eczema. Aloe vera teas contain compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals. This helps to reduce the severity of eczema symptoms, promoting healthier and more balanced skin. Regular consumption of aloe vera teas may also help to improve skin hydration and elasticity.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Aloe vera's anthraquinone glycoside aloin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce skin inflammation and soothe eczema symptoms.
Chrysophanic acid: This triterpenoid compound found in Aloe vera has potent anti-inflammatory and anti-itching properties, which can help alleviate eczema symptoms such as redness and itching.
Acemannan: A type of polysaccharide present in Aloe vera, acemannan has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune system and reduce eczema inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part of Aloe vera for tea, as they contain the highest concentration of gel and other compounds that help soothe eczema symptoms.
Flowers: Aloe vera flowers are also used to make teas, as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce eczema inflammation.
Stems: Aloe vera stems are sometimes used to make teas, as they contain a higher concentration of calcium and other minerals that help to soothe and calm eczema skin.
remedy_recipe: Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water before handling the aloe vera leaves.
Cut 20-30 grams of aloe vera leaves from a mature plant and remove the skin.
Chop the aloe vera gel into small pieces and combine with 1 liter of boiling water.
Allow the aloe vera mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth.
Mix the aloe vera tea with honey and lemon juice to taste and enjoy immediately.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, can reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema, providing relief from itching and discomfort.
Antioxidant property: Chamomile's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate skin damage caused by eczema, promoting wound healing and reducing the appearance of scars.

Soothing and calming property: The flavonoids present in chamomile tea, such as apigenin and kaempferol, have a sedative effect on the skin, calming irritated areas and promoting relaxation, which can help reduce eczema symptoms like itching and restlessness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, teas helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in chamomile tea have been shown to reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema. The tea's calming effects also help to reduce stress, which is often a trigger for eczema flare-ups. Additionally, chamomile's antiseptic properties can help to prevent infections that can exacerbate eczema symptoms, promoting overall skin health and reducing the severity of eczema symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps with eczema by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate symptoms of the condition.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and calm irritated skin associated with eczema.
α-Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce redness, itching, and bacterial infections that often accompany eczema.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the primary part used due to their high apigenin and chamazulene content, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: They are also used to make teas for eczema as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Seeds: Although less common, seeds are sometimes used in teas for eczema as they contain sesquiterpenes that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the flowers of matricaria chamomilla in the morning after the dew has dried.
Rinse the flowers with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the flavors to infuse.
Strain the tea and serve warm or allow it to cool before consuming.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion tea's flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in dandelion tea, including vitamins C and E, help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eczema symptoms.
Immunomodulatory properties: Dandelion tea's saponins have been shown to modulate the immune system, reducing inflammation and suppressing the overactive immune response that contributes to eczema.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, teas helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids are believed to reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritation, which are common symptoms of eczema. Additionally, dandelion tea has been shown to promote the removal of toxins and excess fluids from the skin, helping to reduce redness and itching associated with eczema. This natural remedy may provide relief and support for those seeking a holistic approach to managing eczema symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Luteolin: A flavonoid that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with eczema.
Taraxasterol: A triterpenoid saponin that has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and mediators, thereby reducing the severity of eczema symptoms.
Kaempferol: A flavonoid that exhibits anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation, itching, and skin damage associated with eczema.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high content of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm eczema symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote skin health.
Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale are also used, particularly for their flavonoid and carotenoid content, which can help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing in eczema-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in the early morning or late afternoon when moisture levels are low.
Rinse the harvested taraxacum officinale parts gently with cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of dried taraxacum officinale flowers or 3-4 teaspoons of fresh leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the taraxacum officinale mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the desired flavor and potency are reached.
Strain the taraxacum officinale tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids before serving.

remedy_properties: Allantoin: Allantoin is a potent anti-inflammatory compound found in Comfrey tea, which helps to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.

Alkaloids: The alkaloid content in Comfrey tea has been shown to have antiseptic properties, which can help prevent bacterial infections that often exacerbate eczema, while also soothing skin irritation.

Tannins: Tannins in Comfrey tea act as an astringent, helping to reduce inflammation and itching by shrinking the skin's capillaries and reducing fluid buildup, making it less conducive to eczema flare-ups.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale, also known as comfrey, teas helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's unique combination of allantoin and other compounds helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with eczema. Comfrey tea has been traditionally used to promote skin health and accelerate wound healing, which can be beneficial for individuals suffering from eczema. Its natural properties may also help to reduce itchiness and promote a more balanced skin environment.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Symphytum officinale, such as symphytin and symphytine, possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in eczema-prone skin.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Symphytum officinale, including ferulic acid and caffeic acid, exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which help in neutralizing free radicals, reducing skin inflammation, and promoting skin health.

Saponins: Saponins present in Symphytum officinale possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help in calming irritated skin, reducing itching and redness associated with eczema, and promoting skin hydration.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Symphytum officinale are used to make teas for eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Roots: The roots of Symphytum officinale are used to make teas for eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Symphytum officinale are used to make teas for eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm skin irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 ounces of fresh symphytum officinale leaves and flowers at their peak potency and dry them immediately.
Crush 1-2 teaspoons of dried symphytum officinale into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or grinder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of powdered symphytum officinale in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove any remaining plant particles.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature and serve immediately to preserve its potency and flavor.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Plantain tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aucubin and plaiquinone, which help reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in plantain tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate eczema symptoms like redness and swelling.

Sothistic and protective effects on skin tissue: Plantain tea's soothing and protective properties help calm irritated skin and prevent further damage, allowing the skin to heal and recover from eczema.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantago major, also known as plantain, teas helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves contain compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itching associated with eczema. The tea's anti-oxidant properties also help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting skin health and reducing inflammation. Additionally, plantain tea has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions due to its antimicrobial properties, making it a natural and effective remedy for eczema sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with eczema by scavenging free radicals and inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Apolipoprotein A-1 (ApoA-1) mimetic peptides: May help alleviate eczema symptoms by modulating the skin's barrier function and reducing inflammation, although more research is needed to confirm its effects.
Saponins: Possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can help soothe and calm eczema-prone skin by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm eczema symptoms.
Seeds: Contain plant sterols and other compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, reducing eczema inflammation.
Stems: Contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce eczema symptoms and promote skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of dried plantago major leaves and flowers from a clean area.
Steep the plantago major leaves and flowers in 1 quart of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl.
Add honey to taste to the tea mixture and stir well to dissolve the sweetness.
Store the tea mixture in a glass bottle and refrigerate for up to 3 days.

remedy_properties: Flavonoids: St John's Wort tea contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce skin inflammation and oxidative stress associated with eczema.
Hyperforin: Hyperforin, a unique compound found in St John's Wort, has been shown to possess immunomodulatory effects, potentially modulating the immune response that contributes to eczema symptoms.

Chalcones: The chalcone content in St John's Wort tea may help reduce allergic reactions and inflammation by inhibiting histamine release and mast cell activation, common factors contributing to eczema exacerbations.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, teas helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant contains hyperforin and hypericin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations, making it a popular natural remedy for eczema sufferers. The tea has also been found to improve skin barrier function and reduce redness and itching associated with eczema, providing relief and promoting overall skin health. Its natural anti-inflammatory properties help to calm and heal eczema-prone skin.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with eczema.
Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate eczema symptoms by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

Naphthodianthrones (e.g., Hypericin): Naphthodianthrones have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce the severity of eczema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing eczema symptoms.
Flowers: They are a good source of hyperforin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce eczema inflammation and promote healing.
Stems: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to calm and soothe eczema-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the hypericum perforatum flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated for best results.
Dry the flowers in a single layer on paper bags at room temperature for 1 week.
Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers for a standard infusion serving size.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to serve the infusion at the desired temperature.

remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with eczema, promoting skin health and reducing symptoms.
Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in milk thistle tea have been shown to have anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate itchy and irritated skin caused by eczema.
Saponins: Saponins in milk thistle tea may help reduce inflammation and improve skin barrier function, which can lead to improved skin hydration, reduced itching, and a decrease in eczema symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum, also known as milk thistle, teas helps with eczema because it is rich in flavonoids and silymarin. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe irritated skin and reduce inflammation. The tea's ability to calm and protect the skin makes it an effective remedy for eczema sufferers. Additionally, silymarin helps to repair damaged skin cells and promote healthy skin regeneration, leading to improved skin texture and reduced symptoms of eczema. Regular consumption of milk thistle tea may provide relief from eczema symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Silymarin, a mixture of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and quercetin): Flavonoids have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itching associated with eczema.
Silychristin: Silychristin, a sesquiterpene lactone, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema, as well as promote wound healing.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high content of silymarin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties which can help soothe eczema symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce redness and itching associated with eczema.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their calming effects, which can help reduce stress and inflammation that may contribute to eczema flare-ups.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum flowers and leaves in the morning to ensure optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested silybum marianum flowers and leaves in a warm place for 7 to 10 days.
Measure 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried silybum marianum flowers and leaves for every 8 ounces of water.
Steep the dried silybum marianum flowers and leaves in boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea and let it cool to a comfortable temperature before serving and drinking immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian teas helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with the condition. The tea's natural antioxidants also help to soothe itchy skin and prevent further irritation. Additionally, yellow gentian has been shown to have antimicrobial effects, which can combat bacterial infections that often accompany eczema. By drinking this herbal tea regularly, individuals may experience relief from symptoms such as itching, dryness, and discomfort associated with eczema.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Yellow gentian tea's antibacterial properties can help reduce the risk of infection and inflammation associated with eczema, promoting a healthy skin environment.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in yellow gentian tea, such as gentiopicroside, may help reduce redness, itching, and swelling caused by eczema, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: Yellow gentian tea's antioxidant activity can help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate eczema symptoms, protecting the skin from damage and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yellow gentian plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated skin associated with eczema.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the yellow gentian plant contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema, providing relief to affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one cup of fresh or dried gentiana lutea leaves for every two cups of boiling water.
Combine the gentiana lutea leaves and boiling water in a teapot and allow it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids after preparation is complete.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, then drink one to two cups per day as needed.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cleavers teas helps with eczema because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The herb's antioxidant-rich compounds work to reduce redness and swelling, while its antimicrobial qualities help to eliminate bacteria and other microorganisms that can exacerbate the condition. Additionally, Cleavers' natural astringent properties help to dry out weepy sores and reduce itching, providing relief from the discomfort and distress associated with eczema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Cleavers tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema, allowing for faster healing.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidants in cleavers tea help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation, further exacerbating eczema symptoms.
Astringent property: Cleavers tea has astringent properties that help dry up weeping skin, reduce fluid accumulation, and soothe itching, making it an effective topical treatment for eczema.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Cleavers contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin, making them an effective treatment for eczema.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Cleavers have been shown to possess antipruritic properties, helping to reduce itching and scratching associated with eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh galium aparine leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Rinse the collected plant material thoroughly with cool water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine 20g of dried galium aparine herb or 30g of fresh plant material with 250ml of boiling water in a tea infuser.
Steep for 10-15 minutes, allowing the mixture to simmer and release its medicinal properties slowly.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding solids before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena teas helps with eczema because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tea's flavonoids, such as naringenin and hesperidin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, providing relief from itching and redness associated with eczema. Additionally, the tea's antiseptic and antibacterial properties help to prevent infection and promote healing, making it an effective natural remedy for managing mild to moderate eczema symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lemon verbena tea contains flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in lemon verbena tea can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eczema symptoms and promote healing.
Soothing and calming effects: Lemon verbena's volatile oils have a calming and soothing effect on the skin, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that can trigger or worsen eczema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon verbena roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing skin irritations associated with eczema.
Seeds: Lemon verbena seeds contain antiseptic compounds that help to prevent infection and promote healing of eczema lesions.
remedy_recipe: Measure two teaspoons of dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers.
Combine the verbena mixture with one cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes to allow the herbs to infuse fully.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before applying it topically as an eczema treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with eczema because of its calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe itchy skin and reduce redness. The antioxidants present in lemon balm tea help to reduce oxidative stress, a common cause of eczema flare-ups. Additionally, the flavonoids and volatile oils in the tea have been shown to inhibit the release of histamine, a chemical that can exacerbate eczema symptoms. By consuming lemon balm teas regularly, individuals with eczema may experience relief from symptoms and improved overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm tea's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to eczema symptoms like redness and itching.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm tea, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce swelling and irritation associated with eczema, providing relief from itching and discomfort.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Lemon balm tea's calming effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for eczema flare-ups, and promote a sense of relaxation and reduced emotional reactivity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help soothe and calm itchy skin associated with eczema.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant are rich in antioxidants and essential oils that can help reduce inflammation and itching caused by eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn when they are most potent and fragrant.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly under cold running water to remove dirt and debris gently.
Combine one teaspoon of dried melissa officinalis leaves or four teaspoons of fresh leaves with boiling water steeping for 10 minutes carefully.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth discarding solids immediately.
Drink the warm tea slowly and peacefully three times a day as needed soothingly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with eczema because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's antioxidants also help to reduce redness and itching, providing relief from the discomfort associated with eczema. Furthermore, the calming effects of lavender promote relaxation and reduce stress, which are common triggers for eczema flare-ups. By drinking English lavender teas regularly, individuals can experience a significant reduction in eczema symptoms, leading to improved overall skin health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lavender tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which can help reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema.

Antioxidants: English lavender tea is rich in antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, common triggers of eczema flare-ups.

Soothing and calming effects: Lavender's calming properties can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which are often associated with eczema flares; this tea can also soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itching.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin associated with eczema.

Flowers: The flowers are rich in essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce redness and itching caused by eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers and rinse them gently.
Combine the rinsed lavender flowers with 250 milliliters of boiling water for five minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to discard solids.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional ten to fifteen minutes before serving warm.
Consume one to two cups of this herbal tea twice daily for optimal eczema relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover teas helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tea's isoflavones, such as formononetin and biochanin A, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the skin, which can help alleviate symptoms of eczema like itching, redness, and scaling. Additionally, red clover's antioxidants work to neutralize free radicals that can damage the skin and contribute to the development of eczema. By drinking red clover tea, individuals may experience relief from eczema-related discomfort and promote healthier, clearer skin.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover tea contains a type of flavonoid called isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine effects, helping to reduce itching and inflammation associated with eczema.
Saponins: Saponins in red clover tea have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections that can exacerbate eczema and prevent bacterial overgrowth on the skin.
Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogen content of red clover tea may help to reduce inflammation by regulating estrogen levels, which can contribute to improved skin health and reduced symptoms of eczema.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the red clover plant contain isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Flowers: Red clover flowers are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for reducing inflammation and itching associated with eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality trifolium pratense flowers and leaves in the early morning for optimal potency.
Rinse 20-30 grams of fresh trifolium pratense material with cool running water and gently pat dry.
Combine rinsed trifolium pratense with 500ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container and steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids after preparation.
Store the cooled tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator at a temperature below 4°c for up to three days before consumption.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Canadian Thistle tea, such as flavonoids and lignans, can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing itchy and inflamed skin associated with eczema.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried cirsium arvense flowers in quantities of 20-30 grams per serving.
Combine harvested flowers with boiling water at a ratio of 1:4 parts herb to water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the tea.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding solids and retaining liquid.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before drinking within 24 hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod teas helps with eczema because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated skin. Rhyzomes: The underground stem of the goldenrod plant, rhyzomes contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help to calm irritated skin and reduce symptoms of eczema.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried solidago virgaurea leaves and flowers for every eight ounces water.
Boil the water in a pot and add the solidago virgaurea mixture for an infusion time of five minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a teapot or cup to remove solids.
Allow the tea to steep for ten minutes before serving it warm or at room temperature.
Store any remaining tea in the refrigerator and consume it within 24 hours or freeze it for later use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried achillea millefolium flowers in small quantities for optimal potency.
Combine one teaspoon of dried flowers with one cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for ten to fifteen minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean teapot.
Serve the tea warm or at room temperature after a preparation time of approximately twenty-five minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed teas helps with eczema because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's flavonoids and sesquiterpenes work together to reduce redness and swelling, while also providing a barrier against allergens and irritants. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eupatorium perfoliatum leaves and stems in the early morning for optimal potency.
Rinse the harvested material gently with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried eupatorium perfoliatum leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in a large glass teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard the solids to produce a soothing tea infusion.
Consume the cooled tea within 24 hours of preparation, taking 1 cup three times daily to alleviate eczema symptoms.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eczema that are treated with herbal teas are acne, rosacea, and psoriasis. Certain herbs like calendula, chamomile, and peppermint have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation. Teas made from these herbs can be used as a gentle cleanser or as a compress to apply directly to affected areas, providing relief from dryness, itching, and discomfort.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for eczema are natural remedies made from various herbs that help alleviate symptoms of eczema, such as itching, redness, and inflammation. These teas work by soothing and calming the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Examples of beneficial herbal teas include chamomile, calendula, and peppermint, which can be consumed as hot or cold infusions to provide relief. By incorporating these teas into their daily routine, individuals with eczema can experience improved skin health and a reduced frequency of flare-ups, enhancing their overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with eczema is a blend of chamomile, calendula, and lavender. Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated skin, while calendula's antiseptic and antifungal properties promote healing and reduce inflammation. Lavender's calming effects help to reduce stress, which can exacerbate eczema symptoms. Drinking a warm tea made from this combination may help to alleviate eczema symptoms, such as itchiness, redness, and dryness, promoting healthy and balanced skin.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Biomedicine & pharmacotherapy = Biomedecine & pharmacotherapie, herbal teas may be beneficial for eczema due to their potential to down-regulate the Scoring Atopic Dermatitis (SCORAD) index, a key indicator of the severity of eczema. The study, which analyzed the clinical efficacy of various herbal formulations for the treatment of atopic dermatitis (AD), found that oral and/or topical application of natural herbal medicines can exhibit remarkable potential for the treatment of mild-to-severe AD in adults, children, infants, and pregnant women. Specifically, the study observed a reduction in symptoms such as erythematic intensity, pruritus and itching frequency, transepidermal water loss (TEWL), and expression of AD-mediated chemokines in patients treated with herbal formulations.
